sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on bottom green block
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
curr_nodes = curr_seg['nodes']

# Look for changes - especially the green block at (58,19)
for n in curr_nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'p', 'b', 'Y', 'b', 'Y', 'W']:
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b) // len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b) // len(b)
        print(n['color'] + ": px=" + str(n['pixels']) + " c=("+str(cr)+","+str(cc)+")")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"world_model": "Level 1. Top: 4 colored blocks (b, N, Y, p) - 20px each at row 3. Middle: red frame with 4 gray dots. Bottom: 4 colored blocks (N, p, b, Y) - 16px each at row 58. Gray bar at row 53 is a timer/progress bar that shrinks.
goal_model: Match top colors with bottom blocks.
action_model: MOUSE to select/move blocks. SPACE advances timer.
recent_findings: Clicked on bottom green block at (58,19). Board changed.
open_questions: What changed? Need to see diff.
plan: Check what changed after clicking bottom green block.
cross_level_notes: None.
</parameter>
</function>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# After clicking the green bottom block (N), it got a white border (W: px=20 c=(58,19)) while the green block inside is still N: px=16 c=(58,19). This is a selection highlight!

# The bottom blocks are: N(green), p(purple), b(blue), Y(yellow) at row 58

# Let me click on the top N block to match them.
m = {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 3, 'col': 27}
action([m])
</parameter>

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
